content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. You should select a (not necessarily contiguous) subsequence of AAA and reverse it. In other words, if you select a subsequence Ai1,Ai2,…,AiKA_{i_1}, A_{i_2}, \ldots, A_{i_K}Ai1​​,Ai2​​,…,AiK​​ (1≤i1<…<iK≤N1 \le i_1 \lt \ldots \lt i_K \le N1≤i1​<…<iK​≤N), then for each valid jjj, the iji_jij​-th element of the resulting sequence is equal to the iK+1−ji_{K+1-j}iK+1−j​-th element of the original sequence; all other elements are the same as in the original sequence.

In the resulting sequence, you want to calculate the maximum sum of a contiguous subsequence (possibly an empty sequence, with sum 000). Find its maximum possible value and a subsequence which you should select in order to obtain this maximum value. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print two lines.


The first of these lines should contain a single integer ― the maximum possible sum of a contiguous subsequence.
The second line should contain an integer KKK followed by a space and KKK space-separated integers i1,i2,…,iKi_1, i_2, \ldots, i_Ki1​,i2​,…,iK​.
